title: Tests exercise a duplicated router, not app.go's real handlers
evidence: app_test.go:23-158 setupTestRouter re-declares all six routes inline instead of calling a shared route-builder from app.go; the actual handlers in app.go main() (app.go:209-379) are never invoked by any test, which is why test_coverage=0.299 (only the Database methods are covered).
suggestion: Extract route registration into a testable func setupRouter(db *Database) *gin.Engine in app.go and have both main() and the tests call it, so tests cover the production wiring.

title: Unreachable validation branches in PUT handler; partial PUT wipes fields
evidence: app.go:321 and app.go:327 guard `req.Title != "" && strings.TrimSpace(req.Title) == ""` which can never be true; UpdateBook (app.go:155-181) overwrites every column, so a PUT omitting author/year/isbn silently blanks them.
suggestion: Drop the dead branches; either require the full object on PUT or fetch-merge-update to preserve unspecified fields.

title: Test coverage is 29.9% despite build+tests passing
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.299, defect_rate=1.0 — tests run green but cover only the DB layer, not the HTTP handlers (see test-router-dup).
suggestion: Raise coverage by testing the real handlers rather than a duplicated router.

title: run-summary skill not available in this session
evidence: Step 7 (invoke run-summary) could not run — the skill is not registered; no summary/ was produced.
suggestion: Re-run evaluation in an environment where run-summary is available if an architecture summary is needed.
